paper_title: Gamma Spectrometry Analysis of Environmental Samples

methods.description: Procedure for collecting sand and seawater samples from specific locations and depths within the study area.

steps.description: Samples were collected from the recreation zone, where people (children, in particular) have contact with the wet sand on the beach (National Environment Council [CONAMA], 2000)
steps.searchable_summary: collect wet sand samples from recreation zone

steps.description: and in the spreading zone, where morphological changes of the sand occur due to wave action (Pilkey et al., 1993;Suguio, 1992).
steps.searchable_summary: collect sand samples from spreading zone

steps.description: Profile samplings were carried out using collecting tubes, with the goal of identifying the presence of Cs-137 radionuclides in deeper layers.
steps.searchable_summary: perform profile sampling of sand using collecting tubes

steps.description: Seawater was sampled from the surface in front of the profile sampling sites at a distance of approximately 5 m from each collection point in the sand.
steps.searchable_summary: collect surface seawater samples near sand sampling points

steps.description: The sampling points were located on PB, which is used as a recreation zone near the CNAAA discharge point, the short stretch used as a beach next to the discharge point, and the spreading area. The main sampling points near the discharge point are shown in Figure 1 and the coordinates of each sampling point are presented in Table 1.
steps.searchable_summary: document sampling point locations and coordinates
inputs: sampling locations (PB, near discharge, spreading area, Velho Beach)

methods.description: Preparation of seawater samples for direct analysis by gamma spectrometry.

steps.description: About 5 L of seawater was collected from the surface
steps.searchable_summary: collect approximately 5 L of surface seawater

steps.description: filtered with a 0.45 mm filter
steps.searchable_summary: filter seawater sample using 0.45 mm filter

steps.description: and placed in polyethylene bottles.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer filtered seawater to polyethylene bottles

steps.description: These samples were transferred to Marinelli beakers
steps.searchable_summary: transfer samples to Marinelli beakers for analysis

steps.description: sealed
steps.searchable_summary: seal Marinelli beakers containing samples

steps.description: and stored under refrigeration until the radiochemical analysis was performed
steps.searchable_summary: store sealed samples under refrigeration

methods.description: Preparation of seawater samples using ammonium phosphomolybdate (APM) pre-concentration for Cs-137 analysis by gamma spectrometry.

steps.description: About 20 L of seawater was collected from the surface
steps.searchable_summary: collect approximately 20 L of surface seawater

steps.description: filtered with a 0.45 mm filter
steps.searchable_summary: filter seawater sample using 0.45 mm filter

steps.description: and the pH was adjusted to 1.6 using nitric acid.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust pH of filtered seawater to 1.6 using nitric acid

steps.description: Cesium chloride P.A. (0.26 g) was added
steps.searchable_summary: add 0.26 g Cesium chloride P.A. to seawater

steps.description: and the liquid was agitated for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: agitate liquid for 10 minutes

steps.description: Subsequently, APM (4.0 g) was added
steps.searchable_summary: add 4.0 g APM to the liquid

steps.description: the liquid was agitated for 1 h
steps.searchable_summary: agitate liquid for 1 hour

steps.description: and then left to rest for 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: let sample rest for 24 hours

steps.description: The sample was then filtered with a 0.45 mm filter
steps.searchable_summary: filter sample using 0.45 mm filter to collect precipitate

steps.description: and washed with a 1% nitric acid solution.
steps.searchable_summary: wash filter with 1% nitric acid solution

steps.description: After drying at 60-70 °C for 48 h, the filters were counted for 60 000 s
steps.searchable_summary: dry filters at 60-70 °C for 48 hours

methods.description: Preparation of sand samples for direct analysis by gamma spectrometry.

steps.description: Gamma spectrometry was used for direct analysis of the sand samples. ... The Marinelli beakers containing the samples (sand and seawater) were placed in direct contact with the detector.
steps.searchable_summary: place sand samples into Marinelli beakers for direct analysis

methods.description: Procedure for performing gamma spectrometry measurements on prepared sand and seawater samples, including equipment setup, calibration, and data acquisition.

steps.description: The gamma-ray spectrometer was equipped with a high purity germanium detector. This system has a 20% relative efficiency and an energy resolution of 1.8 keV for the Co-60 radionuclide at the reference energy of 1.33 MeV. The coaxial germanium detector was mounted vertically and coupled to a digital 3 keV high voltage source.
steps.searchable_summary: set up gamma-ray spectrometer with HPGe detector

steps.description: The detector was placed inside a thick lead shielding with an inner layer of cast copper to reduce background radiation.
steps.searchable_summary: place detector inside lead and copper shielding

steps.description: The output signal of the detector was directed to a computer equipped with a Model 9660 multichannel analyzer (Canberra).
steps.searchable_summary: connect detector output to multichannel analyzer and computer

steps.description: The Genie-2000 software package from Canberra was used for data acquisition, and in the scanning and nuclide identification modules.
steps.searchable_summary: use Genie-2000 software for data acquisition and analysis

steps.description: The system was calibrated for energy and efficiency in the same geometry as that of the samples. The energy calibration was performed using a certified calibration source with known energies: Co-60, Eγ = 1332.5keV and Cs-137, Eγ = 661.6 keV.
steps.searchable_summary: perform energy calibration using certified sources (Co-60, Cs-137)

steps.description: The energy efficiency of the detector was determined using a standard cocktail made by Eckert & Ziegler Analytics, traceable by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) in the same geometry as that of the samples.
steps.searchable_summary: determine detector efficiency using standard cocktail source
inputs: standard cocktail source (Eckert & Ziegler, NIST traceable)

steps.description: The Marinelli beakers containing the samples (sand and seawater) were placed in direct contact with the detector.
steps.searchable_summary: place sample in Marinelli beaker in contact with detector

steps.description: Each sample was counted for 60 000 s.
steps.searchable_summary: count each sample for 60000 seconds

steps.description: The decays were corrected to July 2012 for both the sand and seawater samples.
steps.searchable_summary: apply decay correction to measured activities

steps.description: Measurements were also carried out with an empty Marinelli beaker, under identical conditions, to determine the background count.
steps.searchable_summary: measure background count using empty Marinelli beaker

steps.description: The background count was subtracted from each sample measurement to obtain the actual activity of the radionuclides.
steps.searchable_summary: subtract background spectrum from sample spectrum

steps.description: To obtain gamma spectrometry measurements for the Cs-137 radionuclide adsorbed by APM, the seawater samples were filtered using a filter of the same dimensions as the standard filter cocktail of radionuclides manufactured by Eckert & Ziegler Analytics. The concentration of the Cs-137 radionuclide was measured directly at 661.6 keV
steps.searchable_summary: measure Cs-137 concentration from APM filters at 661.6 keV
